sn46.6:1.1: See note on SN 2.18:1.2 for this location.
sn46.6:1.2: Kuṇḍaliya (“the ear-ringed”) is not known elsewhere.
sn46.6:3.4: The text uses three synonyms: citta (“mind”), mānasa (“spirit”), and cetasa (“heart”). | Read apatitthīnacitto (“mind unirritated”) per BJT, and the Thai and PTS variants for the parallel passage at Mnd 10:92.7. The verb form patiṭṭhiyati appears in Pvr 17:172.3 with a list of terms for anger. | “Undaunted spirit” (adīnamanasa) is also at Thag 3.9:1.4 and Thag 15.1:11.4. However, the texts waver between reading adīna (“undaunted”) and alīna (“not sluggish”, for which the meaning is established at Snp 1.3:34.2). The parallel to this passage at Mnd 10:92.7 reads alīna in MS, but adīna in Thai and PTS editions. The confusion extends to the commentaries, which for the present passage glosses, “mind not daunted (adīnacitta) by the power of displeasure”, while at Thag 3.9:1.4 they have, “non-overthrown by laziness, etc., not sluggish mind (alīnacitta), not living lazily”. See too dīnamana (“disheartened”) at DN 18:4.17.
